compliance_risks.title: Contractor Misclassification Risk
compliance_risks.client_exposure: Three contractors show employee-like indicators (fixed hours, single client, no business risk). CRA may reclassify all as employees, triggering source deduction arrears plus 10% penalties.
compliance_risks.action: Commission a formal RC4110 worker-status review for all three contractors before next payroll cycle.

compliance_risks.title: Shareholder Loan Overdue
compliance_risks.client_exposure: Director loan of $45,000 is 14 months outstanding, exceeding the one-year repayment window under s.15(2). The full balance is includible in the director’s personal income unless repaid.
compliance_risks.action: Repay or formally restructure the loan immediately; document as a genuine creditor obligation with a signed promissory note and market-rate interest.

compliance_risks.title: Meals & Entertainment Overclaim
compliance_risks.client_exposure: $34,800 in meals and entertainment was claimed in full. Under s.67.1 only 50% ($17,400) is deductible; the excess claim of $17,400 must be reversed.
compliance_risks.action: File a T2 adjustment to reduce the M&E deduction to $17,400 and adjust the tax provision accordingly.

compliance_risks.title: Missing T2200 Declarations
compliance_risks.client_exposure: Employees claiming home-office deductions have no signed T2200 forms on file. CRA will deny all related deductions on audit.
compliance_risks.action: Obtain signed T2200 forms from all directors and employees claiming work-from-home expenses before the personal tax filing deadline.

compliance_risks.title: Vehicle Logbook Gaps
compliance_risks.client_exposure: Vehicle expense claims lack contemporaneous mileage logs, making the business-use percentage unverifiable. CRA will default to 0% business use on audit.
compliance_risks.action: Implement a digital logbook (e.g., MileIQ) immediately and reconstruct a representative 12-week sample log for prior periods.
